title: Introduction
body: Welcome to the Zyplo documentation. This guide will help you understand how to use Zyplo to plan work, track progress, and deliver projects efficiently.

Zyplo is a developer-focused project management platform designed for modern teams that value speed, clarity, and focus. It combines a clean interface with powerful workflows inspired by tools like Linear and Jira.
With Zyplo, teams can organize work into workspaces, break projects into tasks, and visualize progress using a Kanban-style board.
This guide walks you through the core concepts, daily workflows, and productivity features so you can get the most out of Zyplo from day one.

title: Getting Started
body: Getting started with Zyplo is simple and takes only a few minutes. The goal is to set up your workspace, create a project, and start tracking work.
First, create an account and log in to Zyplo using your credentials. After logging in, you will be prompted to create your first workspace.
A workspace represents your team or organization. Inside a workspace, you can create one or more projects depending on how your team is structured.
After creating a project, invite your teammates and assign them appropriate roles so everyone can collaborate effectively.
Finally, create your first task, assign it to a team member, and start moving work forward using the board.

subsections.title: Workspace
body: A workspace is your team’s main space in Zyplo. It acts as a container for all projects, members, and workspace-level settings.
Most teams create one workspace per company or organization. Larger companies may create multiple workspaces for different departments or teams.
Inside a workspace, you can manage members, control permissions, and configure settings that apply to all projects.
Keeping your workspace structure aligned with your team structure helps maintain clarity and avoids permission confusion as your team grows.

subsections.title: Project
body: A project represents a product, service, or codebase that your team is working on. Every project lives inside a workspace.
Projects help you organize work around a specific goal, such as building a feature, launching a product, or maintaining an existing system.
Each project contains tasks, a board view, and an activity history so you can track what is happening over time.
You can customize workflows, use labels, and adjust priorities to match the way your team prefers to work.

subsections.title: Task
body: A task is a unit of work tracked in Zyplo. Tasks represent concrete pieces of work that move your project forward.
Each task has a title and description, and can be assigned to a team member with a specific priority and due date.
As work progresses, tasks move across the board through different statuses, making progress visible to the entire team.
Keeping tasks small, clear, and well-defined helps teams deliver work more predictably and avoid hidden complexity.

subsections.title: The Board
body: The Kanban board provides a real-time visual overview of your project. Tasks are grouped into columns based on their current status.
This visual layout makes it easy to understand what is being worked on, what is blocked, and what has already been completed.
Teams use the board during daily stand-ups and planning sessions to quickly align on priorities and progress.

subsections.title: Columns
body: Columns represent stages of your workflow, such as To Do, In Progress, Review, and Done.
Each column maps to a task status, and you can customize these columns per project to match your team’s process.
When you move a card from one column to another, Zyplo automatically updates the task’s status and activity history.

subsections.title: Cards
body: Each task appears as a card on the board. Cards are designed to show the most important information at a glance.
From the board, you can quickly see who is responsible for what and which tasks are high priority.
Clicking a card opens the full task details, where you can update the description, change the assignee, or adjust priority and status.

title: Command Palette
body: The command palette is a powerful feature that lets you control Zyplo directly from your keyboard.
Press Ctrl + K to open the command palette from anywhere in the app. From there, you can quickly jump to projects, create tasks, and update task status.
This workflow is designed for power users who want to stay focused and avoid unnecessary context switching between mouse and keyboard.
Over time, using the command palette can significantly speed up your daily workflow in Zyplo.

title: Notifications & Activity
body: Zyplo keeps you informed about important changes through notifications and the activity log.
You will receive notifications when tasks are assigned to you, when someone mentions you, or when important updates happen in your projects.
The activity log provides a chronological history of changes, helping you understand what happened and when.
This system ensures that you stay up to date without needing to manually check every project and board.

title: Shortcuts & Tips
body: Using shortcuts and simple best practices can greatly improve your productivity in Zyplo.
Press Ctrl + K to open the command palette and navigate or perform actions quickly.
Use labels to group related tasks and priorities to plan sprints and releases more effectively.
Make it a habit to review your dashboard daily to stay aligned with your team’s goals and current priorities.
